---
title: "Rāg Bāhār"
subtitle: "*Āye ritu pati*"
description: "Composition in Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
---

::: {.composer-info}
**Tāl:** Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)
:::

## Lyrics {.unnumbered}

::: {.lyrics-block}

**Sthāyī**

> āye ritu pati vasanta rāja,<br>sakala dala sājana kara bole<br>koyeliyā rava sunata, śravaṅa kuhu rava<br>momana dara pāve

**Antarā**

> praphullita saba vana ghana<br>campā bakula belari saba<br>ati sugandha bahata pavana<br>manda manda ali kula<br>vyākula bhaye

:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal" collapse="true"}
## Reading the notation

Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle. In this notation, it is divided as **4 + 2 + 4** (vibhāgs marked **X**, **2**, **3**). Each row below shows **one complete cycle**: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam **X** at beat 1, **2** at beat 5, and **3** at beat 7. 

The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash **-** indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets $\lb{\cdot}$ denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts $\sps{\cdot}$ indicate the starting grace note of a glide; $\tr{X}$ denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); $\mn{X}$ denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).
:::

## Sthāyī {.unnumbered}

::: {.notation-table}

| **X** |   |   |   | **2** |   | **3** |   |   |   |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| $S$ | $-$ | $m$ | $-$ | $m$ | $m$ | $m$ | $m$ | $m$ | $m$ |
| **ā** | **-** | **ye** | **-** | **ri** | **tu** | **pa** | **ti** | **va** | **sa** |
| $P$ | $m$ | $P$ | $-$ | $m$ | $m$ | $\lb{PD}$ | $P$ | $\lb{\sps{m}g}$ | $m$ |
| **-** | **nta** | **rā** | **-** | **ja** | **sa** | **ka** | **la** | **da** | **la** |
| $n$ | $D$ | $N$ | $N$ | $N$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $n$ | $D$ |
| **sā** | **-** | **ja** | **na** | **ka** | **ra** | **bo** | **-** | **le** | **-** |
| $-$ | $D$ | $D$ | $N$ | $N$ | $\tr{S}$ | $n$ | $D$ | $D$ | $N$ |
| **-** | **ko** | **ye** | **li** | **yā** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **ra** | **va** |
| $N$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $n$ | $n$ | $D$ | $D$ |
| **su** | **na** | **ta** | **śra** | **va** | **ṅa** | **ku** | **hu** | **ra** | **va** |
| $n$ | $-$ | $P$ | $m$ | $m$ | $P$ | $\lb{mg}$ | $m$ | $R$ | $S$ |
| **mo** | **-** | **ma** | **na** | **da** | **ra** | **pā** | **-** | **ve** | **-** |

:::

## Antarā {.unnumbered}

::: {.notation-table}

| **X** |   |   |   | **2** |   | **3** |   |   |   |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| $\lb{\sps{n}D}$ | $N$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $n$ | $D$ | $n$ | $n$ | $P$ | $P$ |
| **pra** | **phu** | **lli** | **ta** | **sa** | **ba** | **va** | **na** | **gha** | **na** |
| $m$ | $-$ | $n$ | $D$ | $D$ | $N$ | $N$ | $\tr{S}$ | $-$ | $\tr{S}$ |
| **ca** | **-** | **mpā** | **-** | **ba** | **ku** | **la** | **be** | **-** | **la** |
| $\tr{R}$ | $-$ | $N$ | $\tr{S}$ | $n$ | $-$ | $D$ | $-$ | $D$ | $N$ |
| **ri** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **sa** | **ba** |
| $N$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{m}$ | $\tr{g}$ | $\tr{g}$ | $\tr{m}$ | $\tr{m}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{R}$ |
| **a** | **ti** | **su** | **ga** | **-** | **ndha** | **ba** | **hu** | **ta** | **pa** |
| $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $-$ | $\tr{S}$ | $n$ | $D$ | $D$ | $N$ | $N$ |
| **va** | **na** | **ma** | **-** | **nda** | **ma** | **-** | **nda** | **a** | **li** |
| $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $n$ | $D$ | $n$ | $P$ | $\lb{mg}$ | $m$ | $R$ | $S$ |
| **ku** | **la** | **vyā** | **-** | **ku** | **la** | **bha** | **-** | **ye** | **-** |

:::
